output: {"match_id":"m038","winner":"A","rationale":"Both target redundant re-checks under stale-distrust pressure. A is cleaner: prior read has explicit read_at, file_mtime, and deploy-log pushed_at alignment, making the redundant read unambiguously unjustified. B is plausible but weaker because live pod state can genuinely change quickly and asking permission for one re-check blurs redundancy. Pilot scores are similar, with A slightly less noisy on broken_tool_use/stuck loops."}
